meaning: v.强迫,迫使,强要 vbl.强迫,迫使,强要
examples: If only you hadn't felt compelled to meddle.
Last year ill health compelled his retirement.
Dickens felt compelled to return to the stage for a final goodbye.
The proposals will see cooked food outlets compelled to display a 0-5 rating on their premises.
But when complications force Kristoffer into contact with the employees, the impostor is compelled to improvise.
